item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: 122-new.jpg
Author(s): Grdg32, Elogee FishTruck
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by Elogee FishTruck as an edited version of the image below

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 4.0
Title: Casa Perricholi Habitacion principal en Tomayquichua, Ambo, Huanuco..jpg
Author: Grdg32

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the Luxes (lx) provideded by a light source change depending on the distance from said source, "lets off 14-20 lx" should be further explained by adding "in the containment chamber" or changed to another unit (lumen).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, trying to get clear on this one.

+ show block
– hide block

If powered, it does nothing, unless someone goes to sleep within 500 meters of it. Then, this individual will have bouts of insomnia interspersed with weird dreams about canaries telling them to rescue nightlights.

If unpowered, it does nothing, unless someone goes to sleep within 500 meters of it. Then, the sleeping person will become comatose, and a shadow-being will arise from the comatose person. The shadow will then try to get other people within the radius to fall asleep by providing them with comfy pillows and blankets, singing them lullabies, and forcibly administering anti-insomnia medicine.

With each subsequent person to go to sleep within its range while unpowered, the range increases.

Then, there's an incredibly complicated procedure to get it plugged into a generator if the power goes out. This procedure assumes the loss of at least five personnel, because "of the effects of SCP-122-1 instances".

So, they lullaby them to death? Nothing is mentioned about the 122-1 instances doing anything violent, or having the ability to make someone suddenly fall asleep. There has been no indication of any danger posed to anyone who's awake, provided they remain awake. It's never explained how so many people were killed during the 122-1 incident. Or why they assume anyone who gets too close will be lost.

I want to like this. There's a core concept in here that I think has potential. But… the pieces just aren't fitting together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nvm, I just found out it's a reference too Birdhouse in Your Soul

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does the line about the "canary" mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with a lot of sentiment here: I can't figure out what this SCP really does.

What do the shadow people do beside force feed sleep meds?
Do those in range of the SCP when it is unplugged fall asleep immediately?
How does the SCP kill the affected?
How does it spread?
Why are the personnel unrecoverable?

I have questions!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so both SCP-3060, and SCP-2840 are similar to good ol' SCP-122 in that all three articles are various flavors of Sleep Paralysis shenanigans. The former shares a similar mechanism of infection as SCP-122, while the later appears to be a kind of antithesis. As a result, its likely the Foundation would keep 3060 far from SCP-122, while some may propose SCP-2840 as a means of curing SCP-122. Recommending the addition of the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-122 is to be stored in a standard containment chamber, containing a single electrical outlet. No personnel dormitories are to be constructed within 500 m of the containment area. At no time should SCP-122 enter an unpowered state. Several redundant power systems are to be maintained and inspected regularly. In the event of SCP-122-1 manifestation, 35 members of site personnel assigned to enact containment are to be deployed outside the containment chamber. If it becomes hostile, Procedure-99-Renmar is to be enacted.

To this:

SCP-122 is to be stored in a standard containment chamber, containing a single electrical outlet. No personnel dormitories are to be constructed within 500 m of the containment area. At no time should SCP-122 enter an unpowered state. Several redundant power systems are to be maintained and inspected regularly. In the event of SCP-122-1 manifestation, 35 members of site personnel assigned to enact containment are to be deployed outside the containment chamber. If it becomes hostile, Procedure-99-Renmar is to be enacted. Due to the potentially disastrous side effects in the event of cross contamination, at no point are SCP-122 and instances of SCP-3060 to be stored at the same facility.

Changing this:

When in a powered state, SCP-122 will affect the sleep patterns of all subjects within its radius. If a subject awakens from a state of REM sleep while within SCP-122's radius, they will display signs of insomnia, and will complain of unusual dreams. These dreams have been found to cause minor psychological disturbances, and all personnel should be given weekly psychological evaluations. See Incident 122-1.

To this:

When in a powered state, SCP-122 will affect the sleep patterns of all subjects within its radius. If a subject awakens from a state of REM sleep while within SCP-122's radius, they will display signs of insomnia, and will complain of unusual dreams1. These dreams have been found to cause minor psychological disturbances, and all personnel should be given weekly psychological evaluations. See Incident 122-1.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Proposals to determine SCP-2840's effects on these dreams are currently pending approval.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Make a little containment chamber in your soul

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's old but it's still creepy as hell. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A nightlight.

A canary.

Linnell Children's Hospital.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really have no clear idea what the shadow people actually do. They dont apparently kill the affected subjects but later they get violent and kill Foundation staff through unknown means. Between that and the iffy object class Im inclined to downvote…but that canary line really got me. Im easily amused I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea enough to upvote, but I don't see why it's keter. Incident 122-1 refers to 122-1 instances as killing people, despite the article never mentioning that they do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked the idea of a nightlight that actually creates monsters instead of keeping them away.

Nonetheless, the execution is lacking. Either SCP-122's been a victim of uncoordinated editing or the original author didn't have a coherent idea of what they wanted the SCP to do. As others have pointed out, the SCP's abilities don't match up with its containment procedures or with what the SCP has been described doing.

This SCP needs a rewrite to bring it all together properly. Until then -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not feeling it in general, as there is too much that doesn't fit. A shadow will gather people…so the sleepwalker grabs them? or the shadow leaves the sleepwalker and grabs them?

And a shadow monster "applying blankets and pillows" is funny, but doesn't make sense.

I will not vote though. The last line about a canary paid off enough to keep my down vote in check.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't feel Keter. It fails locked-box, so Euclid. Containment doesn't mesh well with effect as far as I can see.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Super k3t3r

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
is "00" a "u" in this case
sorry for late response

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: s00per keter yo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hmm isn't really keter, i should say euclid…
but nice scp +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus, the picture creeps me out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-122 lets off between 14-20 lx.

I believe lm (lumens) is the correct unit for amount light given off by a light. Lux (lx) is for the intensity of the light on a specific surface. I'm not an expert, but I've done some research into it recently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How the hell did these agents identify 112? Out this big ass children's hospital and they picked this little night light

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really agree with that. I don't think that it makes sense. I think the Contaiment procedures need to be rewritten.
Also, I think that some of the information about 122-1 is too vague, mainly when it is describing their behavior. I just didn't get a very clear image of what they do.

Clear up these things and it will be a huge improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is hands down my favorite! I love it so much because it's pretty unique and explains things well. However, my big reason for liking it so much is that I have a huge fear of the dark and the thought of something that's supposed to keep me feeling safe and being Keter as hell. Well….that's terrifying in and of itself. Upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… I think what makes this so dangerous is a little confusing. SCP-122, when unpowered, causes a comatose state and spawns shadows to gather more people into range. When powered, SCP-122 causes insomnia. Do the shadows gather sleeping people or people who are awake? The article doesn't really mention if the shadows do anything hostile besides gathering sleeping people and sleeping aids. I suppose it could be implied, but no real cause for deaths in incident 122-1 are given. Were the deaths in Incident 122-1 due to insomnia, coma, or the shadows acting in a hostile manner? If the shadows acted in a hostile manner, that seems to be missing from the earlier description of the behavior of the shadows.

The containment also seems a bit overkill. Either leave it unplugged, with no shadows within 500m, or plug it in and make sure no one sleeps within 500m.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This post/picture gave me nightmares - bravo! (Though I agree that this seems more like a Euclid class object).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"After all instances of SCP-122-1 have been reduced to the point where entry is safe, five subjects are to enter the containment chamber, and use an electrical extension cord from the generator to return SCP-122 to a powered state. These personnel are to be considered irrecoverable after entering SCP-122's containment chamber, due to its effect."

…Why? This must be a relic of the pre-edit version, since right now it doesn't appear to do anything worse to you if you get closer, nor to do anything bad at all if it's plugged in and you aren't asleep within 500m. I don't even get why the area directly around the nightlight is dangerous enough to need teams of people to address, much less to write off the five dudes who plug it in — if it's goes unpowered, the shadow people arise from the shadows of people who are asleep, right? It doesn't seem to centrally locate them, so you won't have to fight your way past a room full of shadow dudes to get in and plug in the nightlight. So what makes the room with the nightlight any more dangerous than every other room in the radius of effect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've unredacted it, I think this is good enough that it can stand with a reasonable, in-universe crosslink.

it's a link to one of my own articles because I had one that fit and didn't want to butt in on someone else's turf

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Charles: Fred, I need to enact 99-Renmar asap, or we'll all die and I won't make home for dinner and Sarah will be pissed; go bring me those chemical irritants.

Fred goes to the storage room, sees three boxes on the shelf:

  1. Chemical irritants created as a by-product of SCP-914 (8 capsules).
  2. Chemical irritants created as a by-product of SCP-028 (23 capsules).
  3. Chemical irritants created as a by-product of SCP-1600 (Magenta-flavored).

Fred: Well, fuck.

Seriously, through, as long as it doesn't screw things up too badly in-universe, it's the author's call, if you ask me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Three subjects are to be equipped with chemical irritants created as a by-product of SCP-███"

A minor issue, but why did you redact information vital to the containment procedure?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea of a creepy shdow child trying to read a screaming person a lullaby while other shadow people hold the victim down on a matress Awesome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hum. I wonder if this and 1983 are related.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that this is feex, I liek.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whole idea just doesn't really sit well with me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made edits to adjust for this. You and scan are right, it does make more sense the other way around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi. 'Three subjects are to be equipped with chemical irritants created as a by-product of SCP-███, which has been found to have a derogatory effect on SCP-122-1 instances.'

Do you mean inhibitory or negative rather than degoratory here? Though the later sentences ('reduced') sugest the by product out right kills them.

What range can the instances go hunting for more humans?

The instances' behaviour seems counter productive. The scip doesn't (as written) make people go to sleep, it only affects them when they are asleep and in rem state. So it makes senses the instances drag more humans into the range of the scip, but why then destroy sleeping aids? Wouldn't they hold them hostage until they fall asleep from exhaustion? Whoops, didn't see Scantron raised this

In fact that is the bit that is hardest for me to suspend disbelief - they someohow know what aids sleep. That could be a positive. Leave a million different novel compounds in range of an instance and blammo, instant selection of those that help sleep. Think of the drug R&D that could be saved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this before, and I like it more now that it doesn't sound like it was written in 2008.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They will attempt to locate as many human subjects as possible, and expose them to SCP-122's effect.

What does this entail, exactly? Just bringing them inside the range of SCP-122?

And if they want people to be affected by it, why do 122-1 instances destroy things that make it easier to fall asleep?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like the new rewrite a lot better than the original. Not 100% certain it should be keter, but it's too large of an improvement over the original for that to be a huge problem.
+1

item.title: REWRITE
item.content:encoded: This entry has been heavily rewritten. Please take the time to check it out, with all its re-writey goodness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go ahead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coming back to it, I'd like to seek permission to rewrite this one. I think I can save it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ideas and writing presented here were probably very unique when it was posted, but subsequent works have definitely overshadowed it. The effect is done better in 781, and it just amounts to an ott boring drag of an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamnit. I was gonna try my hand at a long crit, see if I could pull it off, but Safari ate it and crashed my phone. Suffice it to say that I had a more.elaborated version of Scantron's opinion, and would like this more if the cross-test was gone, the bullshit radiation effect got removed, and 781 didn't do the Elm Street bit much better. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Critique in response to the call for review:

+ show block
– hide block

Object Class: Keter

Doesn't seem like this should be Keter. Euclid more like. "A Keter classification indicates that not only is this subject capable of inflicting devastating harm to human life and civilization, but that containment protocols must be extensive, involved, and precisely followed in order to prevent it from doing so." This one doesn't seem to fit the bill.

A security team of no fewer than eight (8) are to be present at all times, and O5 authorization will be required for any direct access.

This seems waaaay over the top. If you keep this think plugged in and people don't sleep near it, you're good. And why would we need to pester O5 every time someone wants to to touch it?

Analysis of the gaseous substance within the bulb has found similarities to the phenomenon of ectoplasm.

This is completely meaningless.

The bulb itself is inseparable from the body of the device.

Pointless indestructibility/inability to take it apart.

SCP-122 lets off a modest degree of light

We can actually measure how much light something produces. A precise reading would look a lot better than "a modest degree", which is unscientific and means nothing.

a long harsh shadow

What the hell does a "harsh shadow" look like?

SCP-122 will release gamma radiation exponentially

So it releases a level of gamma radiation that increases exponentially over a given period of time? If so, there has to be a less clumsy way of phrasing this.

Removal of SCP-122 to prevent SCP-122-1 from breaching containment is performed remotely, to avoid further deaths by radiation poisoning. Inevitably, SCP-122-1 will attempt to sabotage this device, and both incidents where it was successful led to over ██ casualties among staff.

How often are people falling asleep right next to this thing?

massive radiation poisoning

Is this even a thing?

Addendum 122-A: A request to attempt to use SCP-781 to control SCP-122-1 is currently pending approval by O5-█.

This is a horrible fucking idea, and anyone with two braincells could have told you something bad would happen. No way the Foundation approves a test like that, seeing as how they're not stupid. Also, the whole "exposing crazy reality-defying objects to each other" thing.

This article has lots of problems, and the "scary thing what kills you/fucks with you while you're sleeping" trope feels overrepresented on this site to the point where there's other articles that pull off this schtick far better.

-1.

EDIT: Oh yes, and this also has a self-upvote.

item.title: SO
item.content:encoded:
1. If you don't plug it in, it radiations until you plug it in. This may or may not kill everybody ever.
  2. If you plug it in and go asleep, it turns your shadows into your dreams which are super-strong and then they kill you.
  3. Crosstesting.

Yeah, I'm really not feeling the appeal here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I question the 'Keter' classification, it seems that keeping it powered and away from sleeping people effectively contains it.

When someone is asleep within the radius of SCP-122

I assume that is the radius of illumination, perhaps it should be quantified, although I like the rule of 500 meters. If the light does not shine on the sleeper will the shadow appear?

I am also confused over a minor point,

It was presumed that an attending nurse removed SCP-122 from its socket after the death of the child, leading to the release of radiation…Removal of SCP-122 to prevent SCP-122-1 from breaching containment is performed remotely, to avoid further deaths by radiation poisoning. Inevitably, SCP-122-1 will attempt to sabotage this device

Does the shadow guard the nightlight after it kills the sleeper, or does it attempt to breach containment? Doesn't removal of the nightlight start the radiation? Can't the guards simply have a switch to turn the light off, then back on?

At first I thought the shadow unplugged the light to cause the radiation, but otherwise it has to stay near it to protect its own existance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely. Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's the right pic, then?
I wasn't certain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! It's back! =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All hail Yoric, king of SCP images!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently the domain of the original image expired recently and the picture is unavailable. So…unless somebody has an extra copy, we'll need a new nightlight photo. (Thanks to Kens for bringing this up).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took out the references to an SCP deleted in the mass edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno how it escaped notice for so long, but fixed the lead-that-should-have-been-led.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had moved this from Site-19 to Area-02, because I don't think anything Keter would be kept at Site-19. Another one I don't think I need to revert before the Mass Edit (but, hey, I could be wrong).

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
So the room must listen to me
Filibuster vigilantly
My name is shooting star
One note spelled D-I-E!

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
I've got a secret to tell
From my electrical well
It's a simple message
And I'm leaving out
the whistles and bells.

I'm going to kill you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that you went with a night-light for this.

It's sort of like They Might be Giant's "Birdhouse in Your Soul" only it's evil.
